01 / UX & UI Design

IKEA App — UX/UI Redesign

Analyzed the IKEA mobile app through Nielsen's 10 usability heuristics, then proposed and prototyped an improved interface. Each change is documented with a Before / After comparison and linked to a specific UX principle.

Heuristic Evaluation Figma Prototype Mobile UX Cognitive Load
Visual HierarchyRedesigned homepage to eliminate promotion clutter, prioritizing search and core user actions.
Recognition > RecallReplaced text-heavy category lists with visual card grids reducing cognitive effort.
Emotional DesignAdded expressive illustrations to empty states to guide users back into the app flow.
Aesthetic & GroupingIntroduced balanced white space and calm colors in account settings for visual comfort.
02 / Mobile App Development · HCI3302

Mawqifi — Smart Parking App

A full peer-to-peer parking sharing platform built in Flutter. Solves urban parking congestion in Saudi cities by connecting private driveway owners with drivers. 16 operational screens with Nafath SSO, GPS scheduling, digital wallet, and booking management.

Flutter / Dart Figma Prototype 16 Screens Nafath SSO IoT Integration
03 / Inclusive Design · HCI3104

UQU Website — Inclusive Design Audit

Evaluated Umm Al-Qura University's digital portal (Muneer tool) across 8 disability categories. Each barrier was mapped to WCAG 2.1 criteria with concrete improvement proposals including bionic reading mode, pattern overlays for color blindness, and ARIA label fixes.

WCAG 2.1 8 Disability Types ARIA / HTML Screen Reader Testing
👁️
Epilepsy
Seizafe tool + CSS prefers-reduced-motion
🔍
Low Vision
Magnifier + contrast enhancement
🎨
Color Blindness
Muneer filters + pattern overlays
⬛
Blindness
Screen reader + tabindex ARIA fixes
⌨️
Physical
Keyboard-only + voice control
🧠
ADHD
Focus mode + image hiding
🔤
Dyslexia
OpenDyslexic font + reading ruler
💡
Intellectual
Reader mode + text-to-speech
04 / Data Visualization · HCI3401

Data Visualization

Designed persona-driven dashboards for five university stakeholders — from the University President to the Department Head. Each dashboard was tailored to that role's decision context, reading depth, and KPIs. Delivered low-fidelity wireframes alongside high-fidelity Power BI-style visualizations.

05 / Usability Evaluation · HCI3205

Usability Evaluation

Ran a moderated think-aloud usability test with 6 first-year university students on an academic course registration system. Uncovered a 66.7% task failure rate caused by a 60-second session timeout and ambiguous dropdowns. Produced a full report with quantitative metrics, prioritized findings mapped to WCAG, and actionable recommendations.

Think-Aloud Protocol 6 Participants Quantitative Analysis WCAG Referenced
33.3%
Success Rate
50.5s
Avg. Task Time
2.33
Avg. Errors
2.5/5
Satisfaction
Participant Data
UserSuccessTimeErrorsSatisfaction
U1✓ Yes35s14 / 5
U2✗ No60s (Timeout)32 / 5
U3✗ No60s (Timeout)22 / 5
U4✗ No60s (Timeout)31 / 5
U5✗ No60s (Timeout)41 / 5
U6✓ Yes28s15 / 5
Key Findings & Recommendations
HIGH
F1 — Ambiguous Course Names
4/6 users (66.7%) made 2+ errors reading near-identical dropdown entries. Fix: Add instructor name, section, and time slot to each entry.
CRITICAL
F2 — 60-Second Session Timeout
All 4 failures were caused by the timer reaching zero — not user inability. Fix: Extend timeout from 60s to at least 3 minutes.
MEDIUM
F3 — Unclear Course Type Labels
Users hesitated 5–8 seconds on "Regular vs. Special Approval" with no explanation. Fix: Add an (i) tooltip with plain-language descriptions.
06 / Information Architecture · HCI2305

Fasel Plus — Information Architecture Analysis

Evaluated the Information Architecture of Fasel Plus, a free Arabic streaming platform. Identified structural problems and proposed redesign solutions with annotated wireframes — covering search UX, content labeling, navigation, and task flow.

Information Architecture Navigation Design Content Labeling Search UX Task Flow
Redesign Wireframes
Key Problems Identified
🔍 No Fuzzy SearchTypos like "breking bad" return zero results — added "Did you mean…?" prompt.
🏷️ Vague Section Labels"Most Popular" replaced with "Most Viewed in May" for clarity and trust.
📋 Missing Content StatusNo indicator if a series is Completed / Ongoing / Coming Soon.
💳 Poor Subscription FlowRenew & Cancel options redesigned into a single, connected location.
Project Details
CourseInformation Architecture — HCI 2305, UQU
TeamOmar Al-Omari · Nawaf Al-Saeedi · Abdullah Al-Zahrani
SupervisorDr. Mohammed A. Jabali
Platformfaselplus.com — Free Arabic streaming
